system.description: **SPECIAL GIFT FOR REGINA THE THIEF OF HEARTS ONLY.** Confusing flashbacks and painful emotions grip your mind as you explore Wonder. You've been here before as a patient, but the memories are fragmented and unclear. Something terrible happened to you in this place, something Alice Liddell tried to erase. Are you sure you want to remember? Once per Scene, you can attempt to remember something from your forgotten time in Wonder. Choose a specific place (Rabbit Hole, Labyrinth of Doors, Caterpillars' Greenhouse, Basement, Hall of the Mind) or a specific subject (Alice, Hatta, Cheshire, a Patient, the White Rabbits, Wonder itself). The Storyteller will give you one piece of useful information about that place or subject: a hidden Descriptor, a secret Gift they possess, their true Agenda, a weakness, or a crucial detail about how they function. This insider knowledge can be invaluable for navigating Wonder and confronting Alice. However, remembering is intensely painful—these are memories Alice tried to destroy for a reason. For the rest of the Scene after remembering, whenever you use one of your own Gifts, roll 1 die. On a result of 5 or 6, you lose 1 Soma as the mental strain overwhelms you, and you are no longer so sure that you are the Queen. Your identity begins to fragment and blur, just as Alice intended.
system.requirement: Enter and explore any of Wonder's Cure Rooms as Regina the Thief of Hearts, triggering suppressed memories of your previous time as a patient in this bedlam.
system.notes: This Regina-exclusive Gift adds a deeply personal horror element to Wonderbedlam. It reveals that Regina has history with Wonder—she was a patient here, possibly when she was struggling with her own identity and crimes. Alice tried to erase Regina's memories and remake her, but Regina escaped (or was released as 'cured') with her identity intact but fragmented. The once per Scene information gathering is powerful—Regina can learn NPCs' hidden Gifts, secret Agendas, or vulnerabilities by 'remembering' previous encounters. The dice roll penalty (5-6 = lose 1 Soma when using own Gifts) creates tension: use your powers and risk losing yourself, or hold back and survive on mundane actions? The 'no longer sure you are the Queen' effect is narrative—Storytellers can describe Regina questioning her memories, personality, or even her role as a Hunter. Was Regina of Hearts a fake identity Alice gave her? Is the Hunter Regina or someone Alice created? This Gift transforms Wonderbedlam from a rescue mission into Regina's personal nightmare of confronting her own past. Storytellers should drop hints: Patients who recognize Regina, Alice commenting 'I remember you', or Hatta mentioning 'that Queen patient from years ago'. The Gift doesn't cost Soma to activate but the penalty costs Soma over time—reflecting that remembering is slowly destroying Regina's sense of self. This can lead to powerful roleplay: Does Regina want to remember who she was before Alice's treatment? Or is forgetting a mercy?
